overview.tagline: A historic borough where industry meets community in the Meadowlands
overview.description: Carlstadt is a small borough in southeastern Bergen County, straddling the border between residential neighborhoods and the vast Meadowlands industrial district. Founded by German immigrants in the mid-19th century, the borough retains a charming residential section north of Route 120 while its southern portion hosts major warehousing, distribution, and commercial operations. Carlstadt offers affordable Bergen County living with unmatched proximity to MetLife Stadium, American Dream, and major transportation routes.
highlights: Rich German heritage dating to 1849 founding
Adjacent to MetLife Stadium and American Dream complex
Major commercial and industrial hub in the Meadowlands
Affordable housing in a tight-knit residential community
Easy access to NJ Turnpike, Routes 17 and 3

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Carlstadt benefits from substantial commercial ratable base which helps moderate residential tax burdens. Properties assessed by Borough Tax Assessor.
realEstate.recentTrends: The residential market has seen steady growth. Carlstadt's relatively low property taxes (thanks to commercial ratables) and small-town feel attract buyers looking for Bergen County affordability. The housing stock is primarily older single-family homes with some newer townhouse developments.
neighborhoodNotes: The residential section north of Hackensack Street has charming older homes on tree-lined streets
Newer townhouse developments offer updated living options
The Meadowlands industrial area in the south is strictly commercial
Hoboken Road area features a mix of residential and commercial properties

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection provided twice weekly by the borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by street — check borough website
Trash in sealed bags or covered containers by 6:00 AM
50 lb maximum per container
Bulk pickup by appointment with DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Weekly recycling collection through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Commingled recyclables in blue containers
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food waste
BCUA recycling center available for additional items

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water (formerly United Water). Sewer service managed through BCUA.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges on property tax bills
Water main emergencies — call DPW
Annual water quality reports from Veolia

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: Construction and zoning permits handled by the Building Department.
details: Permits required for all construction and renovation work
Commercial and industrial permits handled by same department
Zoning Board meets monthly for variance hearings
CO required for all new construction and changes of use

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Municipal parking lots and metered street parking available in the commercial area.
details: Free parking in municipal lots
Metered parking on Hackensack Street
NJ Transit commuter parking at Williams Avenue station
Overnight street parking requires permit

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers seasonal programs for residents.
details: Youth sports leagues (baseball, soccer, basketball)
Summer camp programs
Senior citizen activities and trips
Holiday community events

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Noise regulations balance residential quiet with the borough's commercial activity.
keyRules: Residential construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM weekdays
Saturday construction 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction Sundays or holidays in residential zones
Industrial zone noise regulated separately under state DEP standards

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Both residential and commercial properties must be maintained to code.
keyRules: Lawns maintained and not exceeding 10 inches
Snow removal required within 24 hours
No junk vehicles or debris accumulation
Commercial properties must maintain facades and parking areas

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Standard pet licensing and control requirements.
keyRules: Dogs must be licensed annually
Leash required when off owner's property
Clean up after pets immediately
Rabies vaccination required for dogs and cats

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking regulated in both residential and commercial zones.
keyRules: No overnight parking on streets without permit
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial vehicle parking restricted in residential areas
Time-limited parking enforced in business district

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental housing must meet borough inspection standards.
keyRules: Rental registration required with the borough
Pre-occupancy inspection for Certificate of Habitability
Landlord responsible for compliance with fire and building codes
Multi-family units subject to additional fire safety requirements

other.title: Meadowlands Commission Regulations
other.summary: Properties in the Meadowlands district are subject to additional regulations from the NJ Sports and Exposition Authority.
keyRules: Development in the Meadowlands zone requires NJSEA review
Environmental impact considerations for wetlands areas
Height and use restrictions specific to Meadowlands zoning
Stormwater management requirements are stricter in flood-prone areas

publicSchools.name: Carlstadt Public School
publicSchools.notes: Single K-8 school serving the borough. High school students attend Becton Regional High School in East Rutherford.

privateSchools.name: St. Joseph School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school in nearby East Rutherford

nearbyHigherEd: Fairleigh Dickinson University (Teaneck and Hackensack campuses)

whyMoveHere: Lower property taxes thanks to strong commercial ratable base
Affordable homes in a charming residential neighborhood
Close to MetLife Stadium, American Dream, and Meadowlands entertainment
Small-town community feel in a metro-accessible location

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit bus or train from nearby stations
commuteToNYC.trainStation: Williams Avenue (NJ Transit Pascack Valley Line, limited service)
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus service available along Route 17. The Williams Avenue station on the Pascack Valley Line offers limited rail service. Many commuters drive to Secaucus Junction for transfer to NJ Transit or PATH trains. The NJ Turnpike and Routes 3 and 17 provide direct highway access.

costOfLiving.summary: Carlstadt offers a cost advantage over many Bergen County towns, with relatively lower property taxes due to its robust commercial and industrial tax base.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Below the Bergen County average for property taxes, near the state average overall
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Above the national average, in line with the northern NJ metropolitan area

pros: Lower property taxes than most Bergen County towns
Adjacent to major sports and entertainment venues
Excellent highway access (NJ Turnpike, Rt 3, Rt 17)
cons: Industrial area in southern portion of borough
Meadowlands area can have odor issues at times
Limited dining and retail in the residential section
Flood risk in low-lying areas near the Meadowlands
Small school district with one K-8 school

restaurants.name: Grasshopper Irish Pub
restaurants.description: Friendly neighborhood pub serving American and Irish comfort food with a great beer selection.

restaurants.name: Carriage House Restaurant
restaurants.description: Upscale American dining in a restored historic building, popular for special occasions.

restaurants.name: Rommel's Ace Hardware & Garden Center Cafe
restaurants.description: Unique local institution combining a hardware store with a community gathering cafe.

restaurants.name: Il Villaggio
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant serving classic pastas, seafood, and brick-oven pizza.

parks.name: Carlstadt Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Community park with playground, athletic fields, walking paths, and memorials honoring veterans.

parks.name: Third Street Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with playground equipment, basketball courts, and open green space.

parks.name: DeKorte Park (Meadowlands)
parks.description: Part of the Meadowlands Environment Center, offering nature trails, bird watching, and wetlands views. Just south of the borough.

events.name: Carlstadt Community Day
events.description: Annual celebration featuring food, rides, games, and live entertainment for the whole community.

events.name: Halloween Parade
events.description: Borough-wide Halloween parade with costumed participants, floats, and prizes.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Community holiday event with tree lighting ceremony, carols, and refreshments.

attractions.name: American Dream
attractions.description: Massive entertainment and retail complex adjacent to Carlstadt featuring Nickelodeon Universe, DreamWorks Water Park, an NHL-sized ice rink, and hundreds of stores.
attractions.address: 1 American Dream Way, East Rutherford, NJ 07073

attractions.name: MetLife Stadium
attractions.description: Home of the NFL's New York Giants and New York Jets, also hosting major concerts and events.
attractions.address: 1 MetLife Stadium Dr, East Rutherford, NJ 07073

attractions.name: Meadowlands Environment Center
attractions.description: Environmental education center in nearby DeKorte Park with exhibits on Meadowlands ecology, trails, and bird-watching platforms.

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the first and third Monday of each month at 8:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Carlstadt Borough Hall, 500 Madison Street, Carlstadt, NJ 07072
